analysis.test: A work is protectable if it bears the imprint of its author's personality, as evidenced by creative choices that are […] and arbitrary — the subjective originality test derived from settled case law, codified at article L112-1 of the Intellectual Property Code. The presumption of authorship (L113-1) does not constitute a presumption of originality: originality must be affirmatively established by the party asserting protection (Cass. civ.1, 7 nov. 2006, n°05-16.843). Originality is assessed by concretely identifying the author's own creative choices, distinct from functional constraints and the common stock of the genre.
analysis.faits: Méridian Studios asserts copyright protection for Mistral against exploitation by Mr. Camille Lefèvre through Quiz Mania. The TJ Paris (Exhibit 12, December 18, 2020, Cassiopée c. Méridian Studios) has already held that 'the originality of the work, which the party invoking protection bears the burden of establishing, requires that it result from [creative] work.' The CA Paris (Exhibit 14, November 10, 2020) confirmed the originality of comparable board games by validating the analytical approach of identifying creative choices. Order […] 2 dated July 24, 2018 (Exhibit 7) and the extract from jedisjeux.net (Exhibit 3) attest to a […] predating the disputed exploitation. The visual screenshots (4).JPG, (5).JPG, (304).JPG, and (716).JPG illustrate the formal elements of the game capable of evidencing the claimed creative choices.
analysis.faille: Absence of an analytical and documented description of the creative choices constituting the originality of Bastille: specific formulation of the […]/challenges, structure and combination of game mechanics, graphic and editorial choices distinctive to the author, separate from the common stock of board games of the 'challenge/[…]' type. Without such an inventory, the court cannot characterize the imprint of the author's personality as required by Cass. civ.1, 7 nov. 2006.
analysis.riposte: Emphasize that originality does not require absolute novelty but rather the demonstration of creative […] and arbitrary choices specific to the author in the combination, formulation, and presentation of the elements (CA Paris, Exhibit 14). Submit the analytical memorandum detailing the specific creative choices made in Bastille (formulation of the challenges, architecture of the categories, visual identity) and distinguish them point by point from the competing games cited. Rely on Cass. civ.1, 30 sept. 2015 (n°14-11.944) […] which validates the assessment of the criteria for characterizing originality.

autorites.title: L112-1 — Code de la propriété intellectuelle
Cass. civ. 1, 05-16.843, 2006-11-07
autorites.summary: Ne caractérise pas l'originalité d'un ouvrage la cour d'appel […] fonde sa décision sur l'absence d'antériorité de toute pièce et le caractère nouveau des choix […] pour sa présentation, ces choix fussent-ils arbitraires, sans caractériser en quoi ces choix portaient l'empreinte de la personnalité des auteurs

autorites.title: Cass. civ. 1, 11-10.463, 2012-04-05
themes: définition de l'étendue de la protection revendiquée et description des produits argués de contrefaçon
définition de l'étendue de la protection revendiquée et description des […] argués de contrefaçon concurrence deloyale ou illicite
action en réparation du chef de la vente de produits contrefaisants
autorites.summary:
Ayant relevé que l'assignation en contrefaçon de […] de bijoux et en concurrence déloyale du chef de la vente des produits contrefaisants, renvoyait simplement aux photographies […] des […] opposés et souverainement estimé que la seule lecture de la liste des pièces jointes ne permettait pas de déterminer la nature et le nombre des articles incriminés, une cour d'appel a pu retenir, d'une part, que les caractéristiques de chacun des […] revendiqués au titre du droit d'auteur n'étaient pas définies et, d'autre part, que les […] argués de contrefaçon n'étaient ni décrits ni même identifiés. 
Elle en a exactement déduit que le […] avait failli dans l'exposé des […] relatifs à la protection dont il sollicitait le bénéfice et aux agissements qu'il incriminait pour rechercher la responsabilité du défendeur au titre d'actes de contrefaçon et de concurrence déloyale

analysis.acte_referme: Analytical memorandum drafted by or with the author/creator of Bastille, describing item by item the creative choices […] […] (selection and formulation of challenges, category architecture, novel combination of mechanics, visual identity), accompanied by a bailiff's report or a time-stamped INPI/IDDN deposit of the protectable elements, and an attestation from the author identifying his personal contribution.
analysis.moyen_adverse: Cassiopée/Julien Mercier will argue that Bastille lacks any independent originality because its elements ([…] themed, challenges […], illustrated cards) belong to the common stock of 'challenge/party' board games (Carto Royale 4, Bluff & Cie, etc.) and that the combination of these generic elements is insufficient to establish the imprint of an author's personality.
analysis.these_adverse: Cassiopée will file Exhibit 10 (TGI Paris, Nov. 26, 2010), Exhibit 22 (TGI Paris, Sept. 29, 2016), and Exhibit 16 (CA Aix-en-Provence, June 21, 2018) to demonstrate that copyright protection for a board game requires a rigorous characterization of originality that the claimant must affirmatively establish, and that mere prior existence or commercial notoriety is insufficient. It will also rely on Exhibit 12 (TJ Paris, Dec. 18, 2020) — rendered in prior litigation involving Méridian Studios itself — to underscore that Méridian Studios failed to establish the originality of Bastille before that court, a prior adverse ruling that directly undermines the present claim. The opposing party will argue therefrom that, in the absence of an analytical inventory of creative choices, the […] must be dismissed for failure of proof.

pieces_adverses.piece: Catalog or rules of prior board games (Carto Royale 4, Bluff & Cie) featuring similar mechanics
pieces_adverses.contre: Irrelevant: similarity of genre does not destroy the originality of the creative choices specific to Bastille in their particular combination; to be contested by means of the comparative analytical memorandum

pieces_adverses.piece: Exhibit 12 (TJ Paris, 18 déc. 2020, Cassiopée c. Méridian Studios) invoked as an adverse precedent against Méridian Studios on the characterization of originality
pieces_adverses.contre: Decision rendered in a different procedural context and without the analytical memorandum having been submitted; the present proceedings afford the opportunity to remedy that evidentiary gap

pieces_adverses.piece: Screenshots or excerpts from third-party games predating Bastille featuring […]/challenges of the same nature
pieces_adverses.contre: Lack of certified date or authenticity; to be contested by bailiff's report (constat d'huissier) and by emphasizing that adoption of a genre does not preclude the originality of the specific combination embodied in Bastille

pieces_attendues: Analytical memorandum from the author/creator of Bastille describing the creative choices […] (formulation of challenges, category architecture, graphic choices).
INPI time-stamped deposit or bailiff's process-verbal (constat d'huissier) of the protectable elements predating the exploitation of Tohu-Bohu
Attestation from the author/creator identifying his personal contribution and the creative decisions made […].
Documented comparative analysis distinguishing the elements of Bastille from the common stock of competing board games (Carto Royale 4, Bluff & Cie).
